_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v2 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=61 with 8 verified claims. '76 witnesses' UNVERIFIED dropped. 'Four days later' (the actual gap is 3-4 days depending on Dec 29 filing or Dec 30 reporting) replaced with 'within days'. 'B40 women-led households' causal claim (UNVERIFIED — Stage 3: vulnerability supported but causal link to 1MDB debt requires further sourcing) softened to 'vulnerable households carry the longer-term fiscal cost'. 'Every major coalition runs on patronage' replaced with 'political-financing reform remains unpassed' (sourced to APPG July 2022 draft). 3R-adjacent (Najib is Malay political figure): editorial framing stays on conviction-and-process rather than on community.

revision_log: REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — '76 witnesses' figure dropped (Stage 3 said witness count requires judiciary press release or court transcript citation).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'four days later' for the appeal → 'within days' (Stage 3: 3 calendar days if Dec 29 filing, 4 if Dec 30 publication).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'RM42 billion paid in 1MDB debt servicing. B40 women-led households feel it in shrinking social safety nets' → 'Malaysia has paid about RM42 billion servicing 1MDB-linked debt. RM24.46 billion remains outstanding.' (separates the supported fiscal-cost claim from the unverified women-led-households causal claim).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fine specified precisely as 'RM11.3879 billion for the four abuse-of-power charges (5× gratification)' rather than ambiguous 'RM11.38 billion'.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED analytical — 'PH, BN, PN, GPS, GRS: every major coalition runs on patronage. None will dismantle it voluntarily' → 'Political-financing reform remains unpassed. A draft bill submitted by APPG MPs in July 2022 was never tabled by the government' (specific, sourced reform-status framing rather than blanket generalisation).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'a draft political financing bill has sat since 2022' specified as 'APPG MPs in July 2022' per Stage 3.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — View 'Vulnerable households, many women-led, carry the cost' → 'Vulnerable households carry the longer-term fiscal cost' (separates supported vulnerability from unverified women-led-households causal link).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): 25 charges, RM2.27 billion, 26 December 2025 verdict, 15 years jail; four Saudi donation letters declared forgeries; RM42B servicing; RM24.46B outstanding; RM9.02B sukuk runs to 2039; fraction of 2026 education budget; political-financing reform unpassed.
